algae_description: Dense, bright yellow-green moss or fine-leafed plant growth carpeting the substrate. Filamentous and feathery in appearance, healthy and vibrant coloration. Some areas appear taller and more established.
substrate_condition: Dark substrate with dense plant coverage; appears well-established with moss or similar plant material throughout. Substrate itself not directly visible due to heavy plant colonization.
concerns: One red/reddish shrimp visible among dense plant growth — excellent sign of successful cycle progression and established conditions. Plant density is robust. Overall conditions appear supportive for the colony.

substrate_condition: clean dark substrate with good distribution
dark substrate with fine grain, clean appearance
algae_description: bright green filamentous/thread algae growing on and around plant stems; appears healthy and actively growing
concerns: Filamentous algae proliferation — moderate growth, not yet blanketing substrate or plants, but visible and spreading. Expected during nitrogen cycle cycling-in phase. Monitor for explosive growth.

substrate_condition: Dark substrate with good detail visibility; appears clean and well-maintained
algae_description: Bright green feathery algae (likely hair algae or similar filamentous growth) covering plants and substrate in the foreground. Dense, healthy growth pattern.
concerns: Moderate to heavy algae bloom — bright green filamentous growth is extensive and visible throughout the foreground

Day 27 of nitrogen cycle: this algae growth is typical during establishment phase when nutrient availability is rising but biofilter maturity is incomplete. Normal but worth monitoring if it accelerates.

algae_description: Dense, feathery bright green filamentous algae (likely hair algae or similar fast-growing variety) covering the foreground. Growth is lush and prominent, indicating nutrient availability during the nitrogen cycle phase.
substrate_condition: Dark substrate with visible texture/pebbles, clean overall with algae growth on planted matter rather than substrate itself
concerns: Significant algae bloom — expected during nitrogen cycle but worth monitoring to prevent overgrowth
Only one shrimp visible in frame; unclear if others are out of view or if colony is smaller than expected at day 27

narrative: A single cherry shrimp — vibrant red coloration — grips a dark plant stem or piece of hardscape in what appears to be a close-up macro shot. The shrimp is active and well-positioned; water is clear. The image is out of focus and tight, making broader tank assessment difficult, but the animal itself appears healthy and mobile.
image_subject: Macro shot of single shrimp on hardscape/plant stem

algae_description: Fine green filamentous algae visible on substrate and lower tank surfaces; appears early-stage growth typical of cycling phase
substrate_condition: Rounded pebble substrate, clean overall with some organic debris settling; fine green algae beginning to colonize. Blue airline tubing visible at top left.
concerns: Image is out of focus/blurry making detailed assessment difficult
Early green algae growth visible — consistent with active nitrogen cycle at day 27
One shrimp visible but image quality limits behavioral assessment
narrative: Aquarium shot from day 27 of the nitrogen cycle cycle shows a single cherry shrimp among rounded pebble substrate. Fine green filamentous algae is beginning to colonize the tank floor and hardscape — a normal and expected sign during active cycling. Water clarity is good, and the shrimp appears present and undisturbed, though camera focus is soft.

Day 27 of the nitrogen cycle: the tank presents a well-established planted scape with thriving bright green moss or stem plants on the left side, a nutrient-rich dark substrate, and active equipment (heater, filter with biological media visible). The water is clear and colorless. No shrimp are visible in this angle, likely sheltered in substrate or plant cover during daytime. The setup appears mature and stable.
substrate_condition: Dark, fine-grain substrate with even distribution and no visible debris. Substrate appears healthy and well-established.

narrative: Day 27 of the nitrogen cycle: the tank is well-stocked with fine-leaved plants and hardscape, predominantly bright yellow-green foliage scattered throughout the substrate and water column. At least one shrimp is visible—a dark/reddish individual in the mid-right area of the tank. The water appears clear despite the rich plant coverage. Substrate shows active cycling and plant colonization, with visible suction cups suggesting recent equipment maintenance or repositioning.
substrate_condition: Dark, fine substrate with extensive plant colonization and scattered bright yellow-green foliage throughout. Several visible suction cup marks and areas of fresh growth.
algae_description: No algae blooms visible. Tank shows healthy aquatic plant growth with no signs of problematic algae overgrowth at this stage of the cycle.
concerns: Early nitrogen cycle phase — ammonia/nitrite still likely present but not visually apparent
Single shrimp visible suggests others may be sheltering during acclimation or early cycle stress
Recent equipment manipulation evident (suction cups) — monitor for any disturbance-related behavioral changes

narrative: Day 27 of nitrogen cycle: A side view of the tank showing substrate, planted flora with yellowed/wilting foliage, no visible shrimp in frame. Water appears clear. Substrate is thoroughly covered with fine dark pellets, appearing clean. A mesh-like structure (possibly an intake guard or decor) is visible on the right. Sunlit ambient conditions suggest natural light exposure through the tank setup.
substrate_condition: Clean, uniformly dark pellet substrate with no visible debris or detritus accumulation
concerns: Plant foliage yellowing and wilting — signs of nutrient stress or nitrogen cycle toxicity
No shrimp visible in shot — may be hiding during cycle stress, typical behavior
Planted mass appears sparse and stressed rather than thriving

image_subject: Container garden or propagation setup with small herb/plant cuttings
narrative: A clear plastic container with drainage holes holds what appears to be a propagation or early growth setup. Multiple small green plant cuttings are visible around the perimeter and edges, growing on what looks like a wooden seed tray or substrate base inside. The setup is photographed outdoors in natural sunlight. This is not the Media Luna shrimp tank — it appears to be a separate propagation or plant nursery project.
concerns: Image shows plant propagation/growth setup, not the shrimp tank
owner_comment: duckweed growth tray today. put it in a sunny spot. hoping to track increase in biomass with a spare esp32 cam.

narrative: Media Luna tank in day 27 of nitrogen cycle: water is noticeably cloudy with a yellow-green tint throughout the column, consistent with bacterial bloom. Bright planted top section with vigorous yellow-green growth visible at water line. Substrate is dark with scattered debris; a mesh feeding dish and what appears to be clay or terra cotta balls (possibly mineral supplements or biofilm media) are visible on the floor. LED lighting bar is mounted and operational. No shrimp observed in frame.
algae_description: Dense yellow-green suspended growth throughout water column; heavy surface-level algae accumulation visible at waterline, likely microalgae bloom typical of nitrogen cycle phase
substrate_condition: Dark substrate with organic debris scattered across floor; mesh feeding dish present, terra cotta or mineral balls visible in lower right area
concerns: Cloudiness consistent with bacterial/microalgal bloom expected during nitrogen cycle day 27
Yellow-green water tint indicates active algae bloom
No shrimp visible — tank is mid-cycle and uninhabited as expected
